mdx.body:
import CodeWrapper from "../../src/components/Mdx/CodeWrapper" import { FontAwesomeIcon } from "@fortawesome/react-fontawesome" import { faSun, faMoon, faBars, faX } from "@fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons" import { faGithub } from "@fortawesome/free-brands-svg-icons" # Overview #### Utility Classes for SCSS I assume you're familar with the concept of utility classes. If not, it's really easy to pick up. I'm trying to think of a quick example for the curious to get started, so how about we look at some colors! ### A quick example: Colors! Here are the default colors the framework defines by default:

Black

White

Red

Green

Blue

Default

Background

Magenta

The docs go mroe in-depth, but let's let's dive into some questions that one might reasonably have on the first encounter: ### "What if I want different colors?" Good Question! I made everything to be (hopefully) easily customizable. Probably one of the first things one would want to do if they had a design would be to set the colors and fonts for their project. Add/remove at will, make your own names, change values, go wild! Instructions are in the [Customize](/customize/) section. ### "What about different shades? Dark-mode? Psuedo classes? For more in-depth coverage of this particular subject I'd glance at the [Pseudo Classes](/docs/pseudo-classes/) overview and move on to the [Colors](/docs/colors/) for examples of shades, pseudo-class functionality, etc. ### "So.. what do these classes look like?" Let's take a look at the HTML for those boxes above: ```html

Black

White

Red

Green

Blue

Default

Background

Magenta

``` Each class name cooresponds to a CSS property. It keeps your stylesheets extremely clean. ### Isn't that more typing than Normal CSS? For sure it's a matter of taste. Everything's a tradeoff. Less style clutter, possibly more typing. But with the almighty ctrl-c, ctrl-v and editor shortcuts, it's not really much of an issue. Plus if you're using a modern frontend framework, you're rarely repeating yourself like this anyway. This is a bit of a special case. The advantages for me personally are incredibly slim stylesheets, I can see the styles right in the markup, everything is still easy to keep consistat, and it's just satisfying! ### "Doesn't already Tailwind do this?" I'm a big admirer of tailwind. But there's differences. See the [Motivation](/motivation/) page! ### "So what exactly do all these classes mean?"" Here are the cooresponding css values for all the classes used in the example above: | Class | CSS rule | | :-------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------- | | bg-black | background-color: #000; | | bg-white | background-color: #fff; | | bg-red | background-color: #cb3810; | | bg-green | background-color: #589f50; | | bg-blue | background-color: #2978a0; | | bg-default | background-color: #252832; | | bg-background | background-color: #fdfcf9; | | bg-magenta | background-color: #bf4081; | | text-white | color: white; | | text-black | color: black; | | text-background | color: #fdfcf9 | | font-large | font-family: "Francois One", sans-serif | | m-xl | margin: 1.5rem; | | m-sm | margin: 0.5rem; | | h-16 | height: 16rem; | | w-6 | width: 6rem | | h-6 | height: 6rem | | display-flex | display: flex; | | flex-col | flex-direction: column; | | flex-wrap | flex-wrap: wrap; | | items-center | align-items: center; | | justify-center | justify-content: center; | | center | display: flex;
align-items: center;
justify-content: center; | Hopefully I will be able do document everything you need. ### "So, how hard is it to change the Colors (or anything)?" Not very hard. Again, see the (Customize)[/customize/] section for more complete examples, but for just a quick overview here's what the colors look like in the \_variables.scss file. If you want a classes for like `bg-purple`, `text-purple`, `border-purple`, etc. for example, it's as simple as adding a key value like `"purple": #662d91` to this map. Here's what the color variables look like by default: ```scss $colors: ( "default": ( "white": #fff, "black": #000, "red": #cb3810, "green": #589f50, "blue": #2978a0, "default": #252832, "background": #fff8f0, "magenta": #bf4081 ), "dark-mode": ( "background": #292d3a, "default": #dcdbda, "green": #246410 ) ); ``` I believe If you can develop a website, you can change certinaly a variable name in a scss file! Why not turn over full control? Of course if you're on like a team of people, making an enterprise site, you might want to hide some of this control, so no one can mess everything up. In that case, probably just use Tailwind, or Bootstrap, or any of the others, or write your own monsterous stylesheets, do whatever you want. This is made for like people who trust themselves to write their own scss, maybe for personal projects, who just want a large number of utility classes, and who want to write their own. I made it for me mostly, but hey anyone can use it! ### "Anything else I should know?"" I hope you liked this quick example. Glance around the docs, see the [Motivation](/motivation/) page if you want more insight into my twisted mind, see the [Install](/install/) page if you want to use these classes, and feel free to click the icon in the top menu bar to go to the repo!
